0000: Mercurial barometer 8 1/2 ft above level of the sea. Returned from hands of Instrument makers, Boston Mass Dec 22 1883 being .06'  high.

Other: 1940: Noticed three slight shocks of earthquake.

People: 0310: At 03:10 cries of help were heard, and a mans head was seen in the  water off the port quater, distance about one cable length. At once lowered the whale boat and pulled in the direction of the cries but was unable to find him. The boat pulled round the probable vacinity for fifteen minutes but saw nothing of him, and the cries having ceased, returned to the ship. Subsequently found the hammock of G.W. Miller (Sea) empty and being unable to find him in the ship, presumed him to have been the man.

Note: 2025: NNW. Land in sight along stbd beam and quarter.

Other: 0800: Dressed ship, British Ensign at the main, with HBMS !Liffey! in honor of the accession of Her Majesty Victoria.

Note: 1600: A marked fall in barometer first hour

Other: 0010: Slight earthquake, shock of about two seconds duration.

People: 1800: While dropping down to hook on, the dinghy was seized by four men, who jumped through the port frd. pivot port, drove the dinghy boys on board and pulled in the direction of the beach to westward of ship. Lowered and sent 1st whale boat in with two officers in charge. Found dinghy deserted on the rocks. Mustered crew and found the missing men were .....etc

0000: No steerage till last hr of watch. Course NNW1/2W
1315: Put over target and engaged firing at target till 1420

People: 2015: In setting top sails ? Booth 1st class boy while laying out the main yard to overhaul top sail sheets lost his footing and fell off the yard but caught with his hands the foot of the sails. Reddick (CFW) WB Mitchell (sea) promptly sprang aloft and seizing him drew him up to the foot rope and assisted him in, theirby preventing his fall overboard and probably saving his life.

Note: 1200: Nearly off San Lorenzo Lt standing for anchorage in Callao Hbr. Noon: Callao, Peru.

People: 1430: Rear Admiral Upsur {Upshur?} visited the ship.  [His records show promotion to Rear Admiral was in October 1884??]

0800: Boarding officer from guard ship reported having come from PSW Steamer from the Northward, the !Lackawana! was in Payta and has on board a number of refugees, belonging to the Inglesias's party.

1000: Learned from the groceryman who attends this ship that all the stores in Callao were closed and that no trains were to be run on either  English or American railroad, that skirmishing had been going on since daylight and that a battle between the Government forces and those of General Caceres was imminent. In consiquence the Captain stopped the regular running boats and no one was allowed to visit the shore for the present.

Noon: An officer was sent to the US Consul to obtain information conserning the condition of Lima and reported all quiet. General Cacares having with his forces retreated to the interior: running of trains resumed between Callao and Lima. By permission of the Capt. Officers area gain allowed to visit the shores.

Other: 1245: Noticed a distinct shock of earthquake, lasting perhaps ten seconds, and accompanied by a troubled state of the water near the ship.

Ships: 0600: Hartford, Lackawanna and Wachusett in port

Flora&Fauna: 1800: Passed through a large school of whales.

Note: 1700: Got Underway under charge of pilot at 1740 in turning with port helm to head down river ship struck on eastern side of river in 23/4 Fathoms water but backed off.

2245: Grounded on the bar near Green Id, backed strong and with jb and engines were unable to move her. Lowered life boats and sounder around the ship finding her surrounded by about 2 and 2 1/2 fathoms water, bottom of soft sand. Brg Ships Hd SW1/2S, Puna Lt SSE1/4E Easterly, Green Id (North end) W1/4N - Tide running ebb.
